seoTitle: How to Create a Workflow in Slack (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Automate repetitive tasks in Slack using Workflow Builder, from choosing a trigger to publishing a live multi-step automation.
intro: Eliminating repetitive manual tasks like standups or intake requests doesn't require programming knowledge. This guide covers using Slack's Workflow Builder to set a trigger, add steps, and publish an automation your team can use immediately.

steps.heading: Open Workflow Builder
steps.body: Click your workspace name header, then launch Workflow Builder from the tools menu.

steps.heading: Create a new workflow
steps.body: Click 'Create Workflow' in the upper right to open the design portal for a fresh automation.

steps.heading: Choose a starting method
steps.body: Select 'From Scratch' or load a pre-built template depending on how much customization you need.

steps.heading: Set your trigger and add steps
steps.body: Configure what event fires the workflow, such as an emoji reaction or schedule, then click 'Add Step' to insert message actions, forms, or app integrations.

steps.heading: Publish and test
steps.body: Click Publish to deploy the automation, then test it in a review channel to confirm it runs as expected.

faq.question: What kinds of triggers can start a Slack workflow?
faq.answer: Triggers can include emoji reactions, scheduled intervals, someone joining a channel, or an external webhook event.

faq.question: Can a workflow post a message automatically on a schedule?
faq.answer: Yes, scheduled triggers let you automate recurring messages like daily standup reminders.

faq.question: Do I need coding skills to build a workflow?
faq.answer: No, Workflow Builder uses a visual step-by-step interface that doesn't require writing code.

faq.question: Can multiple people manage the same workflow?
faq.answer: Yes, you can assign workflow managers and adjust publication permissions to keep automations organized.
